title: [$250] Full story : “Get paid back from my employer” onboarding flow does not work for BYOC users

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Needs Reproduction
labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @danielrvidal **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): #[Convert](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07HPDRELLD/p1773220736936909?thread_ts=1773201569.695189&cid=C07HPDRELLD) ## Action Performed: ## Description: During onboarding, the “Get paid back from my employer” option does not work well for BYOC (Bring Your Own Company) users who have not yet been invited to their company’s workspace. In observed Track sessions, users are signing up for Expensify because their employer uses it, but they have not yet received a workspace invitation. When these users select “Get paid back from my employer,” the flow does not properly accommodate their situation. Many of these users are simply trying to track expenses or prepare expenses before they are invited, especially if they have a corporate card or are not reimbursed directly through Expensify. Currently, the flow only allows them to skip, instead of allowing them to create a workspace or proceed with expense tracking. ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a new Expensify account without being invited to a workspace 2. During onboarding, select “Get paid back from my employer.” 3. Continue through the onboarding flow ## Expected Result: The onboarding flow should support BYOC users who have not yet been invited. Users should have the option to: Create a workspace, or Continue with “Track and budget expenses” if reimbursement via Expensify is not applicable yet. The flow should not force users to skip. ## Actual Result: The “Get paid back from my employer” flow does not properly support users who have not yet been invited to a workspace. Users are effectively forced to skip the step ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The link for the full stork is in slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07HPDRELLD/p1773220736936909?thread_ts=1773201569.695189&cid=C07HPDRELLD) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** **Reproducible in production?:** **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @shawnborton **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Convert](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07HPDRELLD/p1773231200641309) ## Action Performed: ## Description: Approval workflow rows can become difficult to scan when many users are listed. Currently, the names appear as plain text, which makes it harder to quickly identify users and visually parse the workflow. To improve readability, we should introduce a small “user pill” component that includes the user’s avatar alongside their name. This will make the list easier to scan and help users quickly recognize approvers. Additionally, when workflows contain more users than can be displayed, the “+X more” indicator should include a tooltip showing the additional users. Selecting the “+X more” element should continue to behave as it currently does by opening the full Right-Hand Panel (RHP) with the complete workflow details. ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Navigate to Workspace > Workflows 2. Open an approval workflow with multiple users listed 3. Observe the workflow rows containing several approvers ## Expected Result: Approvers are displayed using a user pill component that includes: Small avatar User name This makes workflow rows easier to visually scan. The “+X more” indicator includes a tooltip listing additional users. Clicking or tapping “+X more” opens the Right-Hand Panel (RHP) with the full workflow, as it currently does. ## Actual Result: Approvers are displayed as plain text, which can be difficult to scan when many users are listed. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Distance expense map preview crops start/finish points

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Description In distance expenses, the start and finish points of the route are not visible in the map preview on the expense details page. The map image is rendered in landscape orientation but the receipt preview area is portrait, causing the route endpoints to be cropped/cut off depending on origin/destination. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to a workspace chat 2. Create a track distance map expense 3. Open the expense details page 4. Observe the map preview — the start and/or finish points may not be visible ## Expected Behavior The full route should be visible in the map preview, including start and finish points. ## Actual Behavior The start and finish points are not visible in the map preview because the map image aspect ratio doesn't match the preview container. ## Proposed Solution Display the distance map receipt image like any other receipt (using the 1:1 source map image) instead of giving it special treatment with a landscape crop. The existing receipt display styles should already handle this correctly — we just need to remove whatever special-case handling causes the map to be displayed differently. ## References - Originated from [Web-Expensify PR \#50555 comment](https://github.com/Expensify/Web-Expensify/pull/50555#issuecomment-3950387580) ## Platform Web, Mobile
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @dukenv0307

It should default to the card-level selection in effect at the time the transaction was imported.
